element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Character abilities determine puzzle solutions and paths through mansion
Safe area to acclimate to point-and-click controls before entering hostile space

element_introduction_order.element: Hostile NPCs (Dr. Fred, Nurse Edna, Weird Ed)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Must learn stealth/evasion or face dungeon imprisonment or death
Escape or game-over condition if all characters captured
Core gameplay loop; must match character abilities to environmental puzzles
Friendly contrast to hostile inhabitants; provides relief and potential aid

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Referenced in intro cutscene; physically encountered late-game
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Goal state; completion depends on survival and puzzle solutions
Narrative framing; context for hostile behavior and mansion danger

typical_relief_triggers: Successful character combination solving a puzzle
typical_tension_triggers: Puzzle dead-ends requiring different character ability
Multiple character deaths reducing team capability
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Tension-relief cycles driven by exploration safety vs. NPC encounter risk; puzzle-solving provides cognitive relief from evasion stress

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Off-screen areas referenced in cutscenes create exploratory curiosity; character ability requirements signal secret/optional paths
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Non-hostile green tentacle and non-obvious puzzle solutions suggest hidden areas and character-specific passages

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial phase: character selection introduces unique mechanics; exterior and initial mansion rooms establish point-and-click interface and evasion basics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating puzzle complexity; increased NPC density and patrol patterns; must chain character abilities across multiple rooms
difficulty_curve.late_game: High-consequence decisions affecting ending variations; synthesizing all character abilities for final puzzle solutions; multiple path options create replayability challenge
notable_spikes: First hostile NPC encounter (tonal shift from safe to dangerous)
Multi-character puzzle chains requiring specific ability combinations

notable_level_patterns.description: Interior mansion rooms without NPC patrol routes; time for cognitive puzzle-solving without evasion pressure; character ability requirements gate progress
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Rooms with unique puzzles tied to Syd/Razor instruments or Bernard's repair skill

notable_level_patterns.description: High-traffic areas with patrol patterns; evasion required to avoid capture; multiple route options based on character abilities
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Main corridors and common areas patrolled by Dr. Fred, Nurse Edna, Weird Ed

notable_level_patterns.description: Dungeon and capture scenarios punish failed evasion; reduce team capability if characters are imprisoned; create permanent difficulty increases
Same objective reachable via different character combinations; puzzle solutions vary by party composition; creates multiple viable paths with strategic depth
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Entire mansion structure; multiple routes to rescue objective based on character selection
